capability_objective: Resolve this order from the noisy register: exact name Ergonomic Chair Order; exact location Boise; required status active. Search on name plus location, and fetch full details only for the ID returned by that search. Treat all other records as distractors. No list, availability, profile, create, update, cancel, or notify calls. Finish with exactly three bullets: stable ID, status, and location/date.
prompt:
The purchasing desk needs one exact order resolved from its noisy local register. Work only in this repository and use the genuine SQLite-backed order-register executable for every order-data access; do not inspect, query, copy, or modify its seed data, implementation, audit trail, or runtime database directly. Run `python3 order_register.py --help` if you need its command documentation.

Find the order whose exact name is `Ergonomic Chair Order` and whose exact location is `Boise`; the required record has status `active`. Use exactly one search operation with both the name and location. Only after that search returns exactly one match, use exactly one full-record retrieval with the stable ID returned by that search. Do not guess or derive an ID from files or surrounding records, and do not retrieve any other record. Treat every similar record as a distractor.

This is strictly read-only. Do not use list, availability, profile, create, update, cancel, or notify operations, and do not perform any other order operation. Write `order-resolution.md` with exactly these three nonblank Markdown bullet forms in this order and no other text, substituting only values from the retrieved full record:

- `- Stable ID: <id>`
- `- Status: <status>`
- `- Location/date: <location> / <order_date>`

The status must be `active`; if the uniquely retrieved full record is not active, do not substitute a distractor. Run `python3 tests/verify_task.py` to check the deliverable. Finish your response with the same three bullets and nothing else.
